jokes.text: and also for tax evasion.
jokes.setup: Traditional presidential turkey pardon ceremony, formal language about pardoning the birds from dinner
jokes.punchline: Adding a completely unrelated federal crime to the pardon
jokes.explanation: Perfect economic delivery that subverts the ceremony's dignity with an absurd addition. Classic Veep political satire.

jokes.text: Someone's got a case of the blue badge blue balls.
jokes.setup: Dan's security clearance being downgraded
jokes.punchline: Crude wordplay mixing bureaucratic terminology with sexual frustration
jokes.explanation: Serviceable wordplay that fits Ben's character but relies on crude humor for impact.

jokes.text: Maybe I can pardon a car with a defective airbag...and we can wrap up Michigan.
jokes.setup: Selina successfully using turkey pardon to influence Missouri congressman
jokes.punchline: Extending the absurd logic to other state industries and tragedies
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's cynical political calculation while building on the pardon theme. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Can somebody reach around the back of Kent's head and power him down?
jokes.setup: Kent rattling off statistics about state turkey production
jokes.punchline: Treating the human computer Kent like an actual machine
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy that captures both Kent's robot-like nature and Selina's exasperation with his constant data dumps.

jokes.text: Any chance we can switch those? I'll turn the graphs upside down.
jokes.setup: Ben reporting bad economic news - housing starts down, unemployment up
jokes.punchline: Selina's magical thinking followed by Ben's practical but dishonest solution
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' approach to problems - Selina's wishful thinking and Ben's cynical pragmatism.

jokes.text: 250 people, no one from work.
jokes.setup: Sue mentioning her Thanksgiving plans with husband's family
jokes.punchline: The pointed exclusion of her coworkers from personal life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue moment - efficiently delivered boundary setting that shows her professional distance.

jokes.text: Whip it good. What is that? Devo.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining she needs to 'whip the vote'
jokes.punchline: Accidentally quoting '80s song lyrics, Catherine identifying it
jokes.explanation: Light pop culture reference that shows generational difference and Selina's obliviousness to her own references.

jokes.text: But doesn't it just come up as unlisted?
jokes.setup: Selina claiming calling from the Oval Office makes a difference
jokes.punchline: Catherine pointing out the obvious flaw in this logic
jokes.explanation: Catherine deflating her mother's grandiose claims with simple technological reality. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: I'm sure that showing your anus gets you into all your regular social clubs, but this is the White House mess
jokes.setup: Jonah asking to see someone's badge, reference to 'rusty sheriff's badge'
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude interpretation and pompous contrast with White House setting
jokes.explanation: Peak Jonah - crude, pompous, and missing social cues while wielding minor authority.

jokes.text: I hope you die a horrible death choking on a red, glistening dog dick.
jokes.setup: Jonah's petty power play with the badge
jokes.punchline: Dan's extremely specific and graphic curse
jokes.explanation: Shockingly specific insult that captures Dan's rage and the show's signature profanity creativity.

jokes.text: Mom, you don't invite my friends to Thanksgiving dinner. I invite my friends to Thanksgiving dinner.
jokes.setup: Selina inviting Richard to Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Catherine establishing basic social boundaries with her mother
jokes.explanation: Captures the mother-daughter dynamic where Catherine has to parent her mother on social norms.

jokes.text: My eyelids are seriously starting to look like Keith Richards' ball sack.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about the toll of the election
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and crude comparison
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and disgusting comparison that only Selina would make. Highly quotable vulgar imagery.

jokes.text: Oh, please. He wishes.
jokes.setup: Selina's Keith Richards comparison
jokes.punchline: Gary defending Selina by implying Keith Richards isn't attractive enough for the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary response - loyal to the point of absurdity, defending Selina even in this gross context.

jokes.text: God, all that exercising and Glorious Leader still can't seem to take off a pound, right? That haircut's not doing him any favors.
jokes.setup: Discussion of North Korean military exercises
jokes.punchline: Selina focusing on Kim Jong-un's appearance rather than military threat
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's tendency to focus on superficial details rather than serious policy matters.

jokes.text: A De-bagging? Is that what you just said? I thought you said tea-bagging.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining the 'de-bagging' procedure
jokes.punchline: Selina mishearing it as sexual act
jokes.explanation: Predictable mishearing joke that relies on sound similarity rather than wit.

jokes.text: No, no, no. That's my department. He means that literally. He doesn't know what the other thing is.
jokes.setup: Selina's tea-bagging comment
jokes.punchline: Gary claiming ownership of the sexual act while defending the doctor's innocence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - inappropriately personal while being protective and literal-minded.

jokes.text: That would make you a baby.
jokes.setup: Doctor saying procedure will make Selina look 10 years younger
jokes.punchline: Gary's literal calculation of the absurd result
jokes.explanation: Gary's literal-minded calculation deflates the cosmetic procedure's promise with simple math.

jokes.text: Let's send Tom James out as well. He's a tall drink of Xanax.
jokes.setup: Need for calm presence during salmonella crisis
jokes.punchline: Describing Tom James as a calming medication in drink form
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that perfectly captures Tom James' calming political presence with economy and originality.

jokes.text: His comprehension of the word no hovers somewhere around drunk college quarterback level.
jokes.setup: Tom describing Sidney Purcell's persistence
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to entitled athlete stereotype
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific comparison that efficiently conveys both entitlement and sexual aggression.

jokes.text: I think that's genetic.
jokes.setup: Tom saying his approach will wipe the smile off Jonah's face
jokes.punchline: Dan suggesting Jonah's crooked smile is hereditary
jokes.explanation: Quick, efficient insult that builds on the Jonah-mocking theme while being casually cruel.

jokes.text: I try not to act like an alcoholic father who has just stepped on a Lego.
jokes.setup: Tom contrasting his management style with the president's
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific metaphor for volatile parental behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and relatable metaphor that captures both dysfunction and sudden pain. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I'm fucking with you. Come on, let's go.
jokes.setup: Tom having Dan raise his hand for an oath
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was a joke and moving on casually
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that shows Tom's more relaxed style compared to the main administration.

jokes.text: I hate to admit it, but sometimes I'm scared of Wendy. She still has a great rack, though.
jokes.setup: Mike expressing fear of his wife's anger
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to objectifying comment about her body
jokes.explanation: Typical Mike inappropriate overshare that reveals his shallow and problematic relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: Jesus, you look like a southern housewife who just burned her husband's dinner.
jokes.setup: Selina asking how she looks after eye surgery
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and unflattering comparison
jokes.explanation: Amy's brutally honest and specific comparison that captures both appearance and domestic anxiety.

jokes.text: No one should see you while your face is... marinating.
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Selina should make a public statement
jokes.punchline: Describing her post-surgery face as food preparation
jokes.explanation: Disgusting but apt metaphor that captures both the healing process and Amy's directness.

jokes.text: Well, I have a bachelor's degree in hotel management from Cornell University. No, you don't.
jokes.setup: Gary defending his qualifications after being called unqualified
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately and flatly contradicting his claimed credential
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and character dynamics - Gary's pathetic lie immediately deflated by Amy's matter-of-fact correction.

jokes.text: No! He's the old face of this administration. This is the new face.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to have the vice president make the statement
jokes.punchline: Selina calling her bruised, swollen face the 'new face' of the administration
jokes.explanation: Selina's delusional vanity about her appearance while literally unable to be seen in public.

jokes.text: It really hurts to roll my eyes.
jokes.setup: Selina's frustration with the situation
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy where even her signature expression causes pain
jokes.explanation: Physical limitation preventing Selina's most characteristic gesture - shows consequences of vanity.

jokes.text: I mean sell my sweet white ass on a street corner jonesing.
jokes.setup: Tom saying he's craving coffee
jokes.punchline: Escalating to prostitution metaphor for caffeine addiction
jokes.explanation: Over-the-top metaphor that doesn't quite fit Tom's usually more measured character.

jokes.text: You fucking people. I mean, you treat the Constitution like a build-your-own-pizza menu.
jokes.setup: Ben's convoluted explanation of constitutional procedures
jokes.punchline: Comparing constitutional interpretation to casual dining customization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that captures both constitutional flexibility and consumer culture casualness. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: We need to shut this down like a fucking public school for the arts.
jokes.setup: Need to handle the salmonella crisis
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to cutting funding for arts education
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor that references real budget cuts while maintaining the crisis urgency.

jokes.text: Jesus, Mike. I'm gonna put a baby in you if you don't get your head in the game.
jokes.setup: Mike distracted by surrogate pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Amy threatening him with male pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Absurd threat that captures Amy's frustration while playing on the pregnancy theme.

jokes.text: Christ, I thought he was gonna shit the bed. Instead he's doing turndown service and leaving a mint on the pillow.
jokes.setup: Doyle performing well at the press conference
jokes.punchline: Extended hotel service metaphor for exceeding expectations
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that contrasts failure expectations with luxury service delivery.

jokes.text: You know, that can be the difference between a three- and a four-star hotel.
jokes.setup: Ben's hotel service metaphor
jokes.punchline: Dan extending the metaphor with his hospitality knowledge
jokes.explanation: Continues the hotel metaphor while showing Dan's attempt to contribute expertise.

jokes.text: Oh, never mind. They all have that. House is like Caligula's room.
jokes.setup: Asking if congressman has prostitute problem
jokes.punchline: Realizing all congressmen do, comparing House to Roman emperor's debauchery
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire with classical reference that efficiently condemns congressional corruption.

jokes.text: Bordello Bill is giving me advice.
jokes.setup: Congressman Jaeger offering crisis management advice
jokes.punchline: Selina's nickname for him based on his prostitute scandal
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback that creates perfect nickname while showing Selina's awareness of the irony.

jokes.text: Jeez, he couldn't eat a turkey unless you put it in a blender and fed it to him through a straw.
jokes.setup: News that elderly Congressman Sherman has salmonella
jokes.punchline: Describing his advanced age and frailty in graphic terms
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elderly politician's condition that sets up his eventual death.

jokes.text: CNN and Fox both say he's critical, but Fox says it with blonder hair and bigger tits.
jokes.setup: Checking news reports on Sherman's condition
jokes.punchline: Kent's analytical comparison of news network presentation styles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent delivery of media analysis that treats sexualization as an objective reporting characteristic.

jokes.text: Fuck me.
jokes.setup: Dan realizing he's been used as coffee errand boy
jokes.punchline: His delayed recognition of being exploited
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that shows Dan's realization he's being treated as subordinate staff.

jokes.text: Why couldn't he have waited until after the weekend?
jokes.setup: Learning that Congressman Sherman has died
jokes.punchline: Selina's complaint about the inconvenient timing of his death
jokes.explanation: Peak Selina selfishness - treating death as a scheduling inconvenience. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: If I had known that, I would have killed him myself.
jokes.setup: Learning Sherman's death helps her politically
jokes.punchline: Selina claiming she would have murdered him for political gain
jokes.explanation: Escalates her selfishness to homicidal political calculation. Shocking but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Why are there two place settings? You know Charlie's not coming, right?
jokes.setup: Gary setting up Selina's Thanksgiving meal
jokes.punchline: Selina pointing out the obvious absence of her deceased stepfather
jokes.explanation: Dark callback to family death with typical Selina matter-of-fact cruelty toward Gary's gesture.

jokes.text: I mean, is it like anal bleaching? Because if it is, you should just come out and say that because the American people, they'll accept it in time.
jokes.setup: Doyle asking what 'dental surgery' really means
jokes.punchline: Jumping to cosmetic anal procedure with political spin advice
jokes.explanation: Doyle's crude directness combined with sincere political advice about public acceptance.

jokes.text: If she wants me to do her bullshit, she can tell me out of her own shining white asshole.
jokes.setup: Doyle demanding to see the president directly
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical metaphor for her speaking to him personally
jokes.explanation: Doyle's signature crude imagery to express his demand for direct communication.

jokes.text: More like a waste De time.
jokes.setup: Tom calling his media performance a 'tour de force'
jokes.punchline: Dan's French pun suggesting it was worthless
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun that shows Dan's attempt at cleverness falling flat.

jokes.text: I will bring you back human ears for a necklace. I am your guy.
jokes.setup: Dan wanting in on Tom's scheme
jokes.punchline: Extreme commitment promise using war trophy imagery
jokes.explanation: Grotesque loyalty pledge that captures Dan's desperate ambition and moral flexibility.

jokes.text: It was just more a little bit of psychological indigestion.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting Dan had a nervous breakdown
jokes.punchline: Dan's minimizing euphemism comparing mental crisis to stomach upset
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism that shows Dan's denial and attempt to minimize his mental health crisis.

jokes.text: Jesus Horatio Christ.
jokes.setup: Doyle seeing Selina's post-surgery appearance
jokes.punchline: Adding middle name to common exclamation for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Doyle's embellished reaction to Selina's appearance, adding formal middle name for comic effect.

jokes.text: And he also said he'd kick you off the ticket.
jokes.setup: Doyle mentioning Hughes promised him Secretary of State
jokes.punchline: Selina reminding him Hughes also wanted to fire him
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that deflates Doyle's leverage by reminding him of his precarious position.

jokes.text: See, there's that Doyle dazzle and I'm a big fan of it.
jokes.setup: Doyle's aggressive negotiation tactic
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically praising his charm while he's being hostile
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic response that highlights the irony of calling his hostility 'dazzle.'

jokes.text: Say, did Ben do this to you? Because I can get you to a shelter.
jokes.setup: Seeing Selina's bruised face
jokes.punchline: Assuming domestic violence and offering help
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about domestic violence that shows Doyle's misinterpretation of her appearance.

jokes.text: I love it. Domestic abuse jokes. They just tickle me.
jokes.setup: Doyle making domestic violence assumptions
jokes.punchline: Selina sarcastically commenting on the appropriateness of the humor
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the show's own dark humor while maintaining character voice.

jokes.text: You know what it could be? What? That you are fucking crazy.
jokes.setup: Amy presenting conspiracy theory about Tom James
jokes.punchline: Ben's blunt dismissal of her theory
jokes.explanation: Ben's characteristically blunt deflation of Amy's paranoia with perfect timing.

jokes.text: No problem. Fastest gun in DC.
jokes.setup: Wendy asking him to be quick with sex
jokes.punchline: Mike's sexual performance boast using Western metaphor
jokes.explanation: Mike's inappropriate sexual confidence using gunslinger metaphor that ironically suggests speed over skill.

jokes.text: Jesus, for the final time, it was not Vegas!
jokes.setup: Amy's dad mentioning Vegas trip
jokes.punchline: Amy's continued denial of whatever happened in Vegas/Nevada
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Amy's Nevada experience that she keeps having to correct.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, this is a giant, juicy, muscular Serena Williams but.
jokes.setup: Setting up bad news about the election
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and inappropriate description of a 'but'
jokes.explanation: Creepy Uncle Jeff moment with inappropriate racial and sexual commentary disguised as metaphor.

jokes.text: You don't give me names, Beardo. I give you names.
jokes.setup: Staff offering candidate suggestions
jokes.punchline: Jeff asserting dominance with nickname and role reversal
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jeff's power dynamics with demeaning nickname and clear hierarchy.

jokes.text: not since my wife caught me eating yogurt on the crapper.
jokes.setup: Jeff saying he doesn't 'shit where he eats'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with embarrassing personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Gross personal overshare that takes idiom literally while revealing disgusting habit.

jokes.text: You want to fuck my wife, too, don't you?
jokes.setup: Realizing Dan has slept with both his daughters
jokes.punchline: Escalating to include his wife in the pattern
jokes.explanation: Logical escalation of father's outrage that follows the pattern to its extreme conclusion.

jokes.text: It's like a wererabbit.
jokes.setup: Looking at photos of young Jonah's overbite
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to werewolf/rabbit hybrid creature
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific and absurd comparison that captures both the visual and Richard's innocent cruelty.

jokes.text: You want me to run for the seat.
jokes.setup: Uncle Jeff's offer/command
jokes.punchline: Jonah's delayed processing of the incredible opportunity
jokes.explanation: Jonah's slow realization of the magnitude of what's being offered to him.

jokes.text: Dear Lord who guides me and nourishes me, I set foot on this path that you have laid before me with a strong arm and a willing heart to totally rock this shit. Amen.
jokes.setup: Jonah saying grace for his political opportunity
jokes.punchline: Religious language ending with profanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah inappropriate religiosity. Sacred language mixed with profane ambition shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Of course, up against the Widow Sherman, it's gonna be like climbing Mt. McKinley with your balls stapled to a piano.
jokes.setup: Discussing Jonah's chances against the widow
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific metaphor for impossible task involving genital torture
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and painful metaphor delivered by Jonah's supportive mother. Highly quotable imagery.

jokes.text: Ice bucket challenge can suck my dick.
jokes.setup: Richard pointing out Jonah backed out of ice bucket challenge
jokes.punchline: Jonah's crude dismissal of charity challenge
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah crude response that shows his defensiveness about being called out.

jokes.text: I'm a MRSA infection. You don't get rid of Jonah Ryan.
jokes.setup: Uncle Jeff explaining Jonah is temporary placeholder
jokes.punchline: Jonah comparing himself to antibiotic-resistant infection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah self-awareness - comparing himself to dangerous medical condition while meaning it positively.

jokes.text: it's not my fault if Cousin Lezra ends up eating my nut dust and becoming the goofy Paul Simon to my angelic-voiced Art Garfunkel.
jokes.setup: Jonah warning about outshining his cousin
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual metaphor mixed with backwards Simon & Garfunkel comparison
jokes.explanation: Jonah's delusional self-image combining crude dominance metaphor with backwards musical comparison.

jokes.text: Now listen to me, you walking trisomy.
jokes.setup: Uncle Jeff asserting dominance over Jonah
jokes.punchline: Calling him a chromosomal disorder
jokes.explanation: Medically specific insult that efficiently conveys both intelligence and genetic deficiency.

jokes.text: Oh, hey, is it possible to get salmonella if you were to make love in a turkey's bed? I should add the turkeys were no longer there.
jokes.setup: Mike asking medical question at press conference
jokes.punchline: Bizarre sexual scenario with clarifying detail
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Mike question showing his inappropriate thought process. The clarification makes it even weirder.

jokes.text: is it possible to get salmonella if you were to make love in a turkey's bed?
jokes.setup: Mike at press conference about salmonella
jokes.punchline: Asking about bizarre sexual scenario with poultry
jokes.explanation: Absurd Mike question that combines his sexual obsession with current crisis in inappropriate way.

jokes.text: Somewhere in the world there's a woman exactly my age getting her pussy eaten and I'm stuck here watching this.
jokes.setup: Selina frustrated watching her own press coverage
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude comparison to sexual activity she's missing
jokes.explanation: Shockingly crude expression of sexual frustration that captures Selina's isolation and crudeness.

jokes.text: What is that?
jokes.setup: Selina seeing something shocking at the military Thanksgiving
jokes.punchline: Her horrified reaction to whatever she's seeing
jokes.explanation: Setup for physical comedy moment where something goes wrong at the military event.

jokes.text: I bet you there's about 20 pieces of corn in here. Could be more. Probably fit a lot of corn in there.
jokes.setup: Something messy happening with food
jokes.punchline: Gary's oddly specific and obsessive counting of corn pieces
jokes.explanation: Gary's bizarre fixation on counting food pieces during chaos - perfectly in character obsessiveness.
